mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate increases are positive in >55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which expands corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day forward returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which signals immediate economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with Rank IC dropping below 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates forces central banks to maintain tight financial conditions, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0 during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms sustained economic expansion and corporate profit growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day returns in >65% of quarters, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate strengthening employment conditions and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns occur in <45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a broad-based economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent declines in INDPRO and RSAFS are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of instances during recession onset phases.
